mnemonics.title: Scrum Framework
mnemonics.content: S - Sprint, C - Cross-functional team, R - Roles (Product Owner, Scrum Master, Development Team), U - User stories, M - Meetings (Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective)

mnemonics.title: Agile Principles
mnemonics.content: I - Individuals and interactions, W - Working software, C - Customer collaboration, R - Responding to change

acronyms.expansion: Structured approach to managing and completing complex projects
acronyms.usage: The Scrum framework is widely used in software development and other fields.

acronyms.expansion: Adaptive and iterative approach to software development
acronyms.usage: Agile methodologies emphasize flexibility, collaboration, and rapid delivery.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a rugby scrum, where the team works together to achieve a common goal.
Think of a flexible and adaptive system, where the team responds to change and delivers working software in short iterations.

analogies.analogy: A Scrum Master is like a coach, who facilitates the team and ensures they follow the Scrum framework.
A Product Owner is like a product manager, who defines and prioritizes the product backlog.
